infobox.value: (born 20 March 1981 in [Šiauliai](/wiki/%C5%A0iauliai "Šiauliai")) is a [Lithuanian](/wiki/Lithuania "Lithuania") [long-distance runner](/wiki/Long-distance_runner "Long-distance runner"). She represented her country at the [2008 Olympics](/wiki/2008_Olympics "2008 Olympics") in [Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing"), finishing 37th in the [marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon"). She represented Lithuania again at [London 2012](/wiki/2012_Summer_Olympics "2012 Summer Olympics"), finishing in 27th place.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Lithuanian long-distance runner

Rasa Drazdauskaitė

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Drazdauskaitė at the 2012 Olympics | |
| Personal information | |
| Nationality | [Lithuanian](/wiki/Lithuania "Lithuania") |
| Born | (1981-03-20) 20 March 1981 (age 45) |
| Height | 1.74 m (5 ft 9 in) |
| Weight | 59 kg (130 lb) |
| Sport | |
| Sport | [Running](/wiki/Running "Running") |
| Event | [Marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon") |

**Rasa Drazdauskaitė** (born 20 March 1981 in [Šiauliai](/wiki/%C5%A0iauliai "Šiauliai")) is a [Lithuanian](/wiki/Lithuania "Lithuania") [long-distance runner](/wiki/Long-distance_runner "Long-distance runner"). She represented her country at the [2008 Olympics](/wiki/2008_Olympics "2008 Olympics") in [Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing"), finishing 37th in the [marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon"). She represented Lithuania again at [London 2012](/wiki/2012_Summer_Olympics "2012 Summer Olympics"), finishing in 27th place.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rasa_Drazdauskait%C4%97&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

At the same event ([1500 m](/wiki/2000_World_Junior_Championships_in_Athletics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_1500_metres "2000 World Junior Championships in Athletics – Women's 1500 metres")) in [2000 World Junior Championships in Athletics](/wiki/2000_World_Junior_Championships_in_Athletics "2000 World Junior Championships in Athletics") she finished fifth in 4:21.58.

She ran 4:13.33 at [2001 European Athletics U23 Championships – Women's 1500 metres](/wiki/2001_European_Athletics_U23_Championships_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_1500_metres "2001 European Athletics U23 Championships – Women's 1500 metres") to place 5th.

She also competed in [1500 metres](/wiki/1500_metres "1500 metres") running at the [2002 European Championships in Athletics](/wiki/2002_European_Championships_in_Athletics "2002 European Championships in Athletics") without reaching final.

She was banned from competition from August 2003 to 2005 after having a positive in-competition drug test for the banned steroid [Stanozolol](/wiki/Stanozolol "Stanozolol").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

On her return, she won the 2006 [Vilnius Half Marathon](/wiki/Vilnius_Half_Marathon "Vilnius Half Marathon") and in 2009 she ran a course record time to win at the [Tallinn Half Marathon](/wiki/Tallinn_Half_Marathon "Tallinn Half Marathon").

She also competed indoors over [1500 m](/wiki/2007_European_Athletics_Indoor_Championships_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_1500_metres "2007 European Athletics Indoor Championships – Women's 1500 metres") at the [2007 European Athletics Indoor Championships](/wiki/2007_European_Athletics_Indoor_Championships "2007 European Athletics Indoor Championships"), although she did not get past the heats stage running a time of 4:23.38.

She ran 1:15:00 at [Athletics at the 2009 Summer Universiade – Women's half marathon](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2009_Summer_Universiade_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_half_marathon "Athletics at the 2009 Summer Universiade – Women's half marathon") to place 5th.

At the [2010 European Athletics Championships](/wiki/2010_European_Athletics_Championships "2010 European Athletics Championships") she ran for Lithuania in the women's marathon and placed 15th. She set a race record at the [Athens Classic Marathon](/wiki/Athens_Classic_Marathon "Athens Classic Marathon") in October 2010, completing the race in a time of 2:31:06 to win the race and also the World Military Championship race.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Drazdauskaitė ran at the 2011 [Turin Marathon](/wiki/Turin_Marathon "Turin Marathon") and was the runner-up behind [Yuliya Ruban](/w/index.php?title=Yuliya_Ruban&action=edit&redlink=1 "Yuliya Ruban (page does not exist)") in a personal best time of 2:29:47 hours.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

She finished in 27th place at the [2012 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2012_Summer_Olympics "2012 Summer Olympics") after running a new personal best.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Drazdauskaitė ran 2:31:23 at [2015 World Championships in Athletics – Women's marathon](/wiki/2015_World_Championships_in_Athletics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_marathon "2015 World Championships in Athletics – Women's marathon") to place 11th.

Drazdauskaitė ran 1:11:47 at [2016 European Athletics Championships – Women's half marathon](/wiki/2016_European_Athletics_Championships_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_half_marathon "2016 European Athletics Championships – Women's half marathon") to place 4th.

Drazdauskaitė ran 2:35:50 at [Athletics at the 2016 Summer Olympics – Women's marathon](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2016_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_marathon "Athletics at the 2016 Summer Olympics – Women's marathon") to place 37th.

| Marathon | 2:29:29 hrs |
